title: Making a Rectangle from Two Squares

steps.work: We have two identical squares, each with sides of 3 cm.
steps.explanation: Both squares are the same size, so they will fit together neatly.

steps.instruction: Place the two squares side by side along one edge.
steps.work: We line up the squares so one full side of each square touches the other.
steps.explanation: When two shapes share a full side, they combine into one larger shape with no gaps.

steps.work: The new shape is a rectangle that is 6 cm long and 3 cm wide.
steps.explanation: Two squares side by side create a rectangle. The length doubled (3 + 3 = 6) while the width stayed 3 cm.

title: Building a House Shape from a Square and Triangle

steps.work: We have one square and one triangle. The base of the triangle matches the top side of the square.
steps.explanation: For shapes to combine, the edges that touch must be the same length.

steps.instruction: Place the triangle on top of the square so the base of the triangle sits on the top side of the square.
steps.work: The triangle sits on top of the square, with its base perfectly aligned to the square's top edge.
steps.explanation: The shared edge disappears, and the two shapes become one combined shape.

steps.subgoalLabel: Count the sides of the new shape
steps.instruction: How many sides does the combined shape have?
steps.work: Bottom (1) + left side of square (2) + left side of triangle (3) + right side of triangle (4) + right side of square (5) = 5 sides.
steps.explanation: The shared edge (top of square / base of triangle) is inside the shape, so it doesn't count. The new shape is a pentagon.

steps.instruction: What do we call this combined shape?
steps.work: It is a pentagon (5-sided shape) that looks like a house.
steps.explanation: Combining simple shapes lets us build more complex shapes. A square plus a triangle gave us a pentagon.
